exam_format: Computer-based or paper test at testing center; most states allow the relevant plumbing code book (IPC or UPC) as a reference during the exam.

testing_centers.notes: PSI Exams administers most national and many state plumber licensing exams. Exams taken at PSI testing centers; no online proctoring for plumber license exams.
Pearson VUE administers some state-specific plumbing license exams.
Some states (e.g., Texas TSBPE, NYC DOB) administer exams directly through their own offices or contracted test centers.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name on ID must match registration exactly.
exam_rules: Open-book allowed in most states (IPC or UPC codebook); approved calculator permitted; government-issued photo ID required. Exams are computer-based or paper-based at a testing center; no online proctoring option for most plumber license exams.
renewal_options: License renewal every 2–3 years; continuing education required (8–16 hours depending on state); plumbing code editions update every 3 years and may require code-update training.

prerequisites.description: Experience with backflow prevention assemblies; field work required for certification

retake_policy.notes: Typically a 30-day waiting period between failed attempts. Most states allow 3–5 attempts per year. Some states require reapplication after multiple consecutive failures.

domains.name: International Plumbing Code (IPC) or Uniform Plumbing Code (UPC)
